2	  "body": "3 imported terms are unsatisfiable due violations of taxon constraints:
FMA:77600
ZFA:0000497
ZFA:0000592

Solution proposed by @gouttegd in #3592 

For the first one (FMA:77600), I think the problematic axiom is

'[epiphyseal tract](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034714)' EquivalentTo '[cranial neuron projection bundle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034713)' and ([innervates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002134) some '[parietal organ](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004869)')

That innervates bit seems too precise. The parietal organ is only a part of the '[pineal complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238)' (a part that does not exist in mammals), but the '[pineal tract](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034715)' (a branching part of the epiphyseal tract) innervates another part of the pineal complex, the '[pineal body](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001905)'.

So if a branch of the epiphyseal tract innervates a distinct part of the pineal complex, then the entire epiphyseal tract clearly innervates more than just the parietal organ.

I believe the axiom should be

'[epiphyseal tract](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034714)' EquivalentTo '[cranial neuron projection bundle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034713)' and ([innervates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002134) some '[pineal complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238)')

As for the other two (the ZFA ones), I think the problem lies with the '[adductor muscle of hip](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011144)'.

That term is logically defined as

'[adductor muscle of hip](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011144)' EquivalentTo '[adductor muscle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011145)' and ('[part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050)' some '[pelvic complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010709)')

but is also explicitly classified as

'[adductor muscle of hip](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011144)' SubClassOf '[innervated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002005)' some '[obturator nerve](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005465)'

This is an instance of a pattern that I call “loose definition, tight classification”, where a logically defined term has a “tight” classification that brings more constraints than what its “loose” definition allows – the problem being that the constraints of the tight classification will then apply to all the terms that fall under the loose definition.

In this instance, the logical definition of adductor muscle of hip makes it valid for all vertebrates (all species that have an '[appendage girdle complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010707)'), but its classification as being innervated by the obturator nerve makes it specific to tetrapods only. And thus the '[superficial pelvic adductor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2000592)', which is intended to apply to teleosts (and as such mapped to ZFA:0000592) becomes also specific to tetrapods, since it is inferred to be a adductor muscle of hip.

So, either:

(A) adductor muscle of hip is intended to apply to tetrapods only, in which case it should not have a logical definition that extends to all vertebrates, or

(B) adductor muscle of hip is intended to apply to all vertebrates, in which case it should not be said to be innervated by a tetrapod-specific nerve.

{
  "body": "3 imported terms are unsatisfiable due violations of taxon constraints:
FMA:77600
ZFA:0000497
ZFA:0000592

Solution proposed by @gouttegd in #3592 

For the first one (FMA:77600), I think the problematic axiom is

'[epiphyseal tract](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034714)' EquivalentTo '[cranial neuron projection bundle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034713)' and ([innervates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002134) some '[parietal organ](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0004869)')

That innervates bit seems too precise. The parietal organ is only a part of the '[pineal complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238)' (a part that does not exist in mammals), but the '[pineal tract](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034715)' (a branching part of the epiphyseal tract) innervates another part of the pineal complex, the '[pineal body](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001905)'.

So if a branch of the epiphyseal tract innervates a distinct part of the pineal complex, then the entire epiphyseal tract clearly innervates more than just the parietal organ.

I believe the axiom should be

'[epiphyseal tract](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034714)' EquivalentTo '[cranial neuron projection bundle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0034713)' and ([innervates](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002134) some '[pineal complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0015238)')

As for the other two (the ZFA ones), I think the problem lies with the '[adductor muscle of hip](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011144)'.

That term is logically defined as

'[adductor muscle of hip](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011144)' EquivalentTo '[adductor muscle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011145)' and ('[part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050)' some '[pelvic complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010709)')

but is also explicitly classified as

'[adductor muscle of hip](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0011144)' SubClassOf '[innervated by](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002005)' some '[obturator nerve](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005465)'

This is an instance of a pattern that I call “loose definition, tight classification”, where a logically defined term has a “tight” classification that brings more constraints than what its “loose” definition allows – the problem being that the constraints of the tight classification will then apply to all the terms that fall under the loose definition.

In this instance, the logical definition of adductor muscle of hip makes it valid for all vertebrates (all species that have an '[appendage girdle complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010707)'), but its classification as being innervated by the obturator nerve makes it specific to tetrapods only. And thus the '[superficial pelvic adductor](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2000592)', which is intended to apply to teleosts (and as such mapped to ZFA:0000592) becomes also specific to tetrapods, since it is inferred to be a adductor muscle of hip.

So, either:

(A) adductor muscle of hip is intended to apply to tetrapods only, in which case it should not have a logical definition that extends to all vertebrates, or

(B) adductor muscle of hip is intended to apply to all vertebrates, in which case it should not be said to be innervated by a tetrapod-specific nerve.

",

  "title": "Revise lofical definition causing violations of taxon constraints",

content.thinking:
Let me understand the issue:

1. There are 3 imported terms that are unsatisfiable due to violations of taxon constraints:
   - FMA:77600
   - ZFA:0000497
   - ZFA:0000592

2. For FMA:77600 (epiphyseal tract):
   - Problem: The logical definition states it innervates 'parietal organ' specifically
   - But the pineal tract (a branch of epiphyseal tract) innervates the pineal body, not the parietal organ
   - The parietal organ is only a part of the pineal complex
   - Solution: Change the logical definition to say it innervates the 'pineal complex' instead of 'parietal organ'

3. For ZFA:0000497 and ZFA:0000592 (related to adductor muscle of hip):
   - Problem: UBERON:0011144 'adductor muscle of hip' has a "loose definition, tight classification" issue
   - Logical definition: part of pelvic complex (valid for all vertebrates)
   - Explicit classification: innervated by obturator nerve (tetrapod-specific)
   - This causes fish terms (ZFA) to become unsatisfiable
   - Solution: Either restrict the logical definition to tetrapods, or remove the tetrapod-specific innervation constraint

[Term]
id: UBERON:0000368
name: adductor brevis
def: "The adductor brevis is a muscle in the thigh situated immediately behind the pectineus and adductor longus. It is somewhat triangular in form, and arises by a narrow origin from the outer surfaces of the superior and inferior rami of the pubis, between the gracilis and obturator externus. Its fibers, passing backward, lateralward, and downward, are inserted, by an aponeurosis, into the line leading from the lesser trochanter to the linea aspera and into the upper part of the linea aspera, immediately behind the pectineus and upper part of the adductor longus.[WP]." [Wikipedia:Adductor_brevis_muscle]

[Term]
id: UBERON:0000370
name: adductor magnus
def: "An adductor muscle that attaches to the pelvic bone and the femur. In humans, the adductor magnus is a large triangular muscle, situated on the medial side of the thigh. The portion which arises from the ischiopubic ramus (a small part of the inferior ramus of the pubis, and the inferior ramus of the ischium) is called the 'adductor magnesius portion', and the portion arising from the tuberosity of the ischium is called the 'hamstring portion'. The hamstring portion is not considered part of the hamstring group of muscles, but it is adjacent to it.[WP,generalized]." [Wikipedia:Adductor_magnus_muscle]

id: UBERON:0015238
name: pineal complex
def: "A cluster in the epithalamus that consists of the pineal body and any associated structures, such as the parapineal gland or the parietal organ. The complex is poorly developed in mammals." [http://orcid.org/0000-0002-6601-2165]

xref: ZFA:0001359
is_a: UBERON:0000477 ! anatomical cluster

relationship: part_of UBERON:0001899 {source="ZFA"} ! epithalamus

content.thinking:
Perfect! Now I can see the full content of both terms. Let me analyze what needs to be changed:

1. **UBERON:0034714 (epiphyseal tract)**:
   - Current: `intersection_of: innervates UBERON:0004869 ! parietal organ`
   - Should be: `intersection_of: innervates UBERON:0015238 ! pineal complex`
   - Also need to update the definition from "innervates the parietal eye" to something more general about the pineal complex

2. **UBERON:0011144 (adductor muscle of hip)**:
   - The problem is: it has a broad logical definition (part of pelvic complex) that applies to all vertebrates, BUT it has a tetrapod-specific innervation constraint (innervated_by obturator nerve)
   - According to the issue, the solution is to remove the tetrapod-specific innervation constraint because:
     - The logical definition makes this applicable to all vertebrates with pelvic complex
     - The innervation constraint makes it tetrapod-specific only
     - This causes fish terms (ZFA) that are inferred to be adductor muscle of hip to become unsatisfiable
   - Solution: Remove the `relationship: innervated_by UBERON:0005465` line

Let me make these edits.

input.content:
## Summary

This PR fixes logical definitions that were causing violations of taxon constraints, making three imported terms unsatisfiable: FMA:77600, ZFA:0000497, and ZFA:0000592.

## Changes Made

### 1. UBERON:0034714 (epiphyseal tract)

**Problem**: The logical definition was too precise, stating the tract innervates only the 'parietal organ' (UBERON:0004869).

**Issue**: The pineal tract (a branching part of the epiphyseal tract) innervates the pineal body, not the parietal organ. Both the parietal organ and pineal body are parts of the pineal complex.

**Solution**: Changed the logical definition:
- **Before**: `intersection_of: innervates UBERON:0004869 ! parietal organ`
- **After**: `intersection_of: innervates UBERON:0015238 ! pineal complex`

Also updated the text definition to reflect this change.

### 2. UBERON:0011144 (adductor muscle of hip)

**Problem**: "Loose definition, tight classification" anti-pattern - the term has a broad logical definition that applies to all vertebrates, but an explicit classification constraint that's tetrapod-specific.

**Issue**: 
- Logical definition: `part_of UBERON:0010709 ! pelvic complex` (applies to all vertebrates)
- Explicit constraint: `innervated_by UBERON:0005465 ! obturator nerve` (tetrapod-specific)
- This caused fish terms like 'superficial pelvic adductor' (UBERON:2000592, mapped to ZFA:0000592) to become unsatisfiable because they were inferred to be adductor muscles of hip, but fish don't have obturator nerves.

**Solution**: Removed the tetrapod-specific innervation constraint:
- **Removed**: `relationship: innervated_by UBERON:0005465 {notes="a small part of adductor magnus is innervated by the tibial nerve", source="dbpedia"} ! obturator nerve`

## Validation

- Used `obo-checkout.pl` and `obo-checkin.pl` to edit terms following project guidelines
- Verified changes were correctly applied to src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo
- Both fixes follow the solutions proposed by @gouttegd in issue #3592

## Checklist

- [x] Identified the problematic logical definitions
- [x] Checked out terms for editing using obo-checkout.pl
- [x] Modified UBERON:0034714 to use broader pineal complex constraint
- [x] Modified UBERON:0011144 to remove tetrapod-specific innervation
- [x] Checked in changes using obo-checkin.pl
- [x] Verified changes in main ontology file
- [x] Committed with clear description and @dragon-ai-agent signature

## Impact

These changes should resolve the unsatisfiability of:
- FMA:77600 (related to epiphyseal tract)
- ZFA:0000497 (related to adductor muscle constraints)
- ZFA:0000592 (superficial pelvic adductor)

The fixes maintain the semantic integrity of the ontology while allowing proper cross-species application of these anatomical terms.
